summary: Joel Spolsky announces that Fog Creek Software is finally moving into their new office space after over a year of planning and construction since March 2007. The new office is twice as large, in a better neighborhood, and features premium amenities including adjustable-height desks, aquariums, and a marble-lined shower. He notes the frustrating delays typical of office build-outs, including moveable walls from Haworth that won't arrive until six weeks after move-in. The post is a brief, lighthearted update teasing more details to come.
key_points: Fog Creek's new office took over 18 months from planning to move-in, illustrating how long commercial build-outs take
The company is investing heavily in workspace quality — 5x rent increase for better finishes, ergonomic desks, and premium amenities
Even with careful planning, vendor delays (Haworth moveable walls) forced them to move in without walls and doors
Joel continues his philosophy that developer workspace quality matters, investing in ergonomics and environment
money_quotes: costing about 5 times as much in rent
adjustable-height desks (for developer ergonomics, of course)
we're literally going to have to move in without walls and doors, which will arrive about 6 weeks late
key_insight: Fog Creek's willingness to pay 5x more rent for a premium developer workspace reflects Joel's long-held belief that investing in work environment is essential to attracting and retaining top talent.
